meta.setting: back-road trail-mark cluster near pine timber and mining-country brush
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in navigation evidence and false-route tension

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Back-road movement through mining, logging, and ranch country.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Gold-rush movement and settlement material context.

image_prompt.subject: an axe-blazed pine, bent nail, small cairn, branded stake, chalk smear on stone, and boot tracks where a back trail splits
image_prompt.scene: overcast trail edge after rain; one rider crouches to compare marks while another watches the wrong fork, no readable symbols or text
image_prompt.composition: square evidence scene with marks large in foreground, forked trail mid-frame, and brushy timber road disappearing beyond
image_prompt.camera: low tracker-view angle close to bark cuts, nail, stones, and muddy tracks
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the marks plain, useful, and suspicious instead of decorative.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine trail-evidence scene
render_traits: slightly simplified riders, trail fork, tree bark, stones, stake, nail, and brush
modest texture resolution on bark, chalk, iron, wet dirt, wood, leather, and brush
hard ambient occlusion under stones, roots, boots, stake, nail head, and brush
cool post-rain light with wet bark highlights
believable 1899 trail marks, riding gear, and route evidence
playable navigation scene where false direction and tracking matter
style_modifiers.palette: wet pine brown, chalk white, iron black, mud umber, bark gray, brush green, leather brown, and overcast blue.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable symbols, letters, brands, signs, labels, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The axe blaze, bent nail, cairn, branded stake, chalk smear, trail fork, rider, and tracks are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to rugged 1899 trail navigation.
The image supports navigation, pursuit, false-mark, warning, and tracking scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
